title: Notes on joy
content:
## My summer

This summer, I have embraced the joys in my life, from friends to food. I am spending my days in leisure. I have taken up sailing—an expensive endeavor, but one that felt right given the opportunity to create lore and to spend time with friends.

After all, what is the point of being rich if you cannot enjoy it?

I have had the opportunity to chat with some very successful people in the last few weeks leading into summer. There was one question I always made sure to ask before parting ways:
> "What is one thing you wish you could have right now, that you cannot get?"

Nearly everyone answered with something along the lines of,
> "I wish I could have more time to enjoy the life that I worked so hard to build."

That is when it hit me: somewhere along the way from studying for the SAT to psetting at Harvard, I lost the plot. That plot being joy. The goal is joy. The reason for success and money is to gain joy, but if you can reach out right now and grab it, why not just do it?

I am doing everything I can to ensure that everyday this summer is filled with joy. I never turn down an opportunity to spend time with people I like.

title: Hello, World
content:
## Starting this blog

I've been meaning to start writing for a long time. This is my attempt to actually do it.

## What I plan to write about

Mostly things related to my research: language models, tokenization, inference, and the occasional field note from the job market or academia.

Some posts will be long and technical. Others will be short observations. I'll try to write things I would have wanted to read when I was earlier in my career.

## Why now

I'm finishing my PhD, which means I have a lot of thoughts to get out of my head and onto paper. Writing is how I figure out what I actually think.

If something here is useful to you, I'd love to hear about it.
